article_title: Updates to liquor licensing process for private corporations

briefs.title: BC Liquor Licensing Now Includes Directors and Officers in Tied House Rules
briefs.summary: BC's liquor regulator updated its rules so that a company's directors and officers are now considered when checking for 'tied house' relationships (situations where a bar, restaurant, or store is financially linked to a specific liquor maker in a way that favours that maker's products). A new online Legal Entity Review process also lets the LCRB formally request corporate documents through the licensing portal.
briefs.action_required: Log into the BC liquor and cannabis licensing portal to report any tied house associations via the Permanent Change to a Licensee application.
briefs.article_title: Updates to liquor licensing process for private corporations
